title: rohdea fimbriata
intro: Rohdea fimbriata, also known as the Chinese squill, is a type of plant belonging to the Asparagaceae family, native to China and Southeast Asia. Traditionally, Rohdea fimbriata has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving cough and sore throat, and aiding in digestion. Rohdea fimbriata is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, and its roots are also used to make medicinal pills and ointments.
